reasoning: The Lean statement captures the informal claim exactly: given a finite group G of order 462, G is not simple. The `[Fintype G]` instance is required merely so that `card G` is defined and equals 462; this is equivalent to the informal `|G| = 462`, which itself implies finiteness. The `[Group G]` typeclass matches the implicit group context of the Dummit-Foote exercise, and `¬ IsSimpleGroup G` is the standard Mathlib predicate for "G is not simple." No hypotheses have been added or removed that would alter the logical content, so the formalization is faithful.
